'''Bask Modl''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]]<ref name="CSWE" /> native of [[Bestine IV/Legends|Bestine IV]],<ref name="TAL" /> a high-[[Gravity/Legends|gravity]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of rocky islands, located in the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]].<ref name="EGtPM">''[[The Essential Guide to Planets and Moons]]''</ref> He served the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] as an [[Republic officer/Legends|officer]] in the [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]] during the [[Mandalorian Wars]]. This war, between the Republic and the resurgent forces of the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]], began in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]], where the [[Mandalorian Neo-Crusaders/Legends|Mandalorians]] made a three-pronged assault against the Republic in [[3963 BBY]].<ref name="TAL" />

In the aftermath of this assault, known by the Republic media as the "Onslaught," Modl received a promotion to the rank of [[Captain/Legends|captain]]<ref name="TAL" /> for his heroic efforts during the fighting.<ref name="CSWE" /> This promotion, reported in the [[The Admiral's List: Jimas Veltraa Memorial Edition|Jimas Veltraa Memorial Edition]] of ''[[The Admiral's List]]'', an official publication of the Republic Navy, occurred alongside that of notable officer [[Saul Karath]] to [[Rear Admiral/Legends|Rear Admiral]]. Modl was a courageous soldier, and consequently recognized by the Republic Navy for his heroic efforts during the Onslaught.<ref name="TAL" />

==Behind the scenes==
Bask Modl was created by [[John Jackson Miller]], the author of the [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic (comic series)|''Knights of the Old Republic'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comics]], and was mentioned in ''[[The Admiral's List: Jimas Veltraa Memorial Edition]]'', appended to [[Knights of the Old Republic 13|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 13]]. This [[in-universe]] news source is designed as "an official communique for the Republic providing news from the 'regular navy' point of view," inspired by the "Napoleonic-era Britain's Naval Chronicle."<ref name="DH Boards">{{DHBoards|url=viewtopic.php?p=143036|thread=News from the world of KOTOR -- literally!|user=[[John Jackson Miller]]|date=2006-12-18}}</ref> Given that Bask Modl is only mentioned in passing, there is no indication of his [[Species/Legends|species]];<ref name="TAL" /> however, Bestine has elsewhere been described as having [[Human/Legends|Human]] colonists, albeit in a period 4,000 years after these events.<ref name="EGtPM" />
